describe: A coherent apartment-move project plus one true stray errand ('Pick up milk and eggs') that has no natural place inside it. Asserts the standalone contract field (docs/llm-pipeline.md, Stage 2): the stray task is marked standalone true, no sectionRef, so src/pipeline/write.js's toProjectTree routes it into a second Inbox tree instead of forcing it into the new project's 'No section' column alongside the real project work.
transcript: I'm moving out of my apartment at the end of the month, so I need to get everything organized. First I need to give my landlord written notice, technically 30 days out, so I should get that in this week. For packing, I want to start with the stuff I don't use often, books and off season clothes, then work up to the kitchen last since I still need it every day. For the actual move, I need to get quotes from a couple of moving truck companies before I book one, and once I pick one I need to reserve the truck for the last Saturday of the month. I also need to cancel the internet at the old place, but not too early, I want to time it so it overlaps with the new place's install date instead of having a gap. And I need to update my address, the post office, my bank, and my license all need the new one. Oh, and the new building requires a parking permit application before I can park there, so I should get that started soon since it apparently takes a couple weeks to process. Somewhere in there I really should just also pick up milk and eggs, keep forgetting.

mockResponse.reasoning: This describes one coherent apartment move: notice, packing, moving logistics, and address updates. Picking up milk and eggs is a real but unrelated errand, so it stays out of the project.

tasks.content: Pack books and off season clothes first
Get quotes from moving truck companies

subtasks.content: Reserve the moving truck for the last Saturday of the month

tasks.content: Cancel internet at the old place, timed to overlap with the new place's install
Update address with the post office
Apply for a parking permit at the new building
